name: Web Contexts Playground
summary: Exercises the Web Contexts standard in a real browser: a declarative <script type="context"> registration, hierarchical resolution through the injector tree, and the strict vs. flexible (claim-based) lookup modes — with a live toggle and a conformance badge per invariant.
description:
Concept

This playground exercises the Web Contexts standard. A parent scope and a child scope each hold a context of the same type (profile) resolved through the injector tree; the child's context only claim()s user.* queries.

What it proves

It demonstrates three surfaces and badges each invariant: (1) declarative registration via <script type="context"> recognised by a scoped CustomContextRegistry; (2) strict lookup always resolving to the closest context (the child, claim() ignored); and (3) flexible lookup (the Most-Flexible-Default, #911) consulting claim() and deferring up-chain to the claiming parent when the child declines. A live mode/query toggle re-resolves and shows which context wins.

Scope

POC playground — applies the webcontexts + webinjectors patches itself (unplugged, non-invasive) and resolves contexts directly; no app shell. Tier 1: declarative declaration, hierarchical resolution, claim-based strict/flexible modes (#1117).
